title: Latest News, Breaking News Today - Entertainment, Cricket, Business, Politics - India Today
meta_description: Check out the latest news from India and around the world. Latest India news on Bollywood, Politics, Business, Cricket, Technology and Travel.
meta_keywords: Latest News, Bollywood News, India News, World News Today, News Headlines, Breaking News Headlines, News Today
text_content: News All except enemy ships can pass Hormuz: Iran after Trump's 48-hour ultimatum The statement comes hours after US President Donald Trump threatened to target Iranian power plants if the waterway does not "fully open" within 48 hours. Strait of Hormuz open to all ships except those linked to 'enemies' US President Trump gives 48-hour deadline to open Strait of Hormuz Iran ready to cooperate with UN maritime organisation to protect ships Global If US targets our nukes, we'll bomb Delhi, Mumbai: Ex-Pak envoy's bizarre remark A controversial remark by a former Pakistani diplomat has triggered concern amid rising regional tensions, with references to India drawing sharp reactions as geopolitical fault lines appear to widen further. advertisement STANDPOINT Deepika Padukone's quiet support for Ranveer Singh: Why social media can't let it be The internet is losing it over Deepika Padukone's silence on Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar 2. But since when did a happy marriage require an Instagram post? The trolling reflects the classic double standard: Demanding wives be public cheerleaders while ignoring their right to a private life. India News 1 killed, 17 injured as ropeway collapses at Chhattisgarh temple during Navratri rush Tragedy strikes during Navratri at Chhattisgarh's Khallari Mata temple as ropeway cable snaps causing casualties Your daily capsule for quick news Anaya Bangar undergoes gender change surgery, thanks father for support More >> Sunday Special I was offered lavender marriage. A closer look revealed the dark reality I was proposed lavender marriage twice within five months. Such moments seemingly unfold daily on matrimonial apps as queer people turn to this arrangement for social camouflage. A woman is offered a life of opulence abroad, but the emotional cost is huge and legal guardrails near-zero. Global Ambling off Air Force One, struggling to sit down: Trump sparks health concerns A brief moment caught on camera has reignited debate around Donald Trump's health, with another clip adding fuel to speculation as online reactions grow sharper and questions around his fitness return. Global Another botched op? Report flags US role in Bahrain blast that injured civilians A version that came within minutes is now being questioned days later. As new evidence emerges from Bahrain, the gap between first claims and fresh findings is raising difficult questions. advertisement India News Russian oil tanker bound for China arrives in India; first of seven vessels Oil tanker Aqua Titan carrying Russian crude arrived at the New Mangaluru port on Saturday evening. The re-routing of such vessels comes after Indian refiners stepped up purchases of Russian oil following the 30-day waiver. Is the Election Commission overstepping or Is Mamata politicising bureaucrat transfers? Two letters sent to the poll panel by the West Bengal CM questions both the exercise of ECI's authority and its intent. Her "All the Best" signoff is, therefore, a weaponising of Bengali politeness. Trends The death of 'lived-in homes' in India In the race to design the "perfect" home, are we losing what made homes feel like ours? US-Israel-Iran War Live: US, Iran trade threats over energy targets as war escalates US-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The conflict in the Middle East witnessed an escalation on Sunday (IST) after a missile struck the city of Dimona, which hosts Israel's main nuclear research facility. This came as Iran said it was targeting Israel's nuclear programme in retaliation for an earlier strike on its nuclear enrichment facility in Natanz. Arad, another city in southern Israel, also came under a missile strike. The two incidents resulted in injuries to more than 100 people, including children. Israel has denied responsibility for the strike on Natanz. Hours after the strikes on Dimona and Arad, the Israeli military said it was conducting a fresh airstrike on Tehran. In another development, US President Donald Trump threatened Iran with strikes on its power plants if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened "within 48 hours." Follow IndiaToday.in for live updates on the Middle East conflict. IPL 2026 Virat Kohli laughs off claims he sought charter flights to London for IPL 2026 IPL 2026: Virat Kohli laughed off claims he sought charter flights to London during IPL 2026, responding to social media rumours ahead of Royal Challengers Bengaluru's season opener in Bengaluru. Global The ace up Iran's sleeve? What is a dirty bomb and will Tehran use one? With uranium enriched to 60%, it is not possible to build a nuclear bomb, but a radiological dispersal device can still be made. Such devices can be used to disperse radiological dust and smoke over a wide area. If Iran can make it, it could make Israel very uncomfortable. advertisement TRENDING TOPICS Rakesh Bedi Navratri Day 4 Air India Love Horoscope Horoscope Today US-Israel-Iran War Live Play Puzzle Games Word! The Daily Play the daily crossword Word on WORD Drop the letters strategically to form words Global Ground report: The war is loud. Beirut's kindness is almost a whisper In a city reshaped by war and displacement, survival in Beirut now rests on quiet acts of care. Each morning, strangers show up — not with answers, but with what little they can carry. Cricket Anaya Bangar undergoes gender change surgery, thanks father for support Anaya Bangar shared that she has successfully undergone gender-affirming surgery in Thailand. She credited her father Sanjay Bangar and family for their support through the emotional journey. Music Justin Bieber, Usher in tense exchange at 98th Oscars after-party: Report The 98th Academy Awards after-party saw a reported clash between Justin Bieber and Usher. Their long-time mentor-protege bond now appears strained, with neither addressing the incident publicly. Global Finding Mojtaba Khamenei: Iran Supreme Leader's whereabouts puzzle US, Israel The continued absence of Mojtaba, according to an Axios report, has been a matter of discussion during President Donald Trump's intelligence briefings. The report also quoted an official as saying that the US national security team is still assessing who is in charge in Tehran. Health A silent imbalance: Women still donate the organs and men get them Handcrafted By Our Editors Four out of five organ donors in India are women, and four out of five people who received the organs were men. The imbalance remains a telling reflection of society itself. India News Question Time: Weekly News Quiz Think you read the headlines this week? Let's test that! Global 6 killed in Qatar after military chopper crashes due to technical glitch The accident came amid heightened tensions in the region, with Iran attacking US bases and other key installations across the kingdom several times since the Israeli-US airstrikes on February 28. Features Elon Musk says China will win AI race, but don't count the US out yet Handcrafted By Our Editors China and the US are racing to outdo each other in AI. But both have different strategies, and while Elon Musk believes that China will win in the long run, it is not yet so cut and dry. Jobs The AI Hiring Hypocrisy: Why AI is okay at work, but 'cheating' in job interviews Companies want AI-driven productivity at work, but penalise it in interviews. That contradiction in hiring practices is now raising questions bigger questions about outdated evaluation systems. Trending News Bengaluru police respond after video of Lamborghini doing stunts goes viral The clip, shared by a user on X, captures a Lamborghini repeatedly doing donuts at a public junction under a flyover in Bengaluru, even as other vehicles pass by. News Nvidia CEO says engineers should be evaluated on basis of how many AI tokens they use Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang says the rise of AI is changing software engineering so much that companies may soon judge engineers by how many AI tokens they use instead of just how much code they write. He believes access to AI compute will become a key productivity tool, and engineers who use more AI effectively will deliver better results. India News It's back to Iranian oil for India after US waiver, but Tehran throws a spanner Refiners in India are planning to resume buying Iranian oil after the US lifted sanctions. It came as energy prices shot up amid the Iran war. It comes on the back of India already snapping up Russian oil currently stranded at sea. LATEST STORIES Ground report: The war is loud.
